purpose: Each mutant is a minimal self-contained input encoding ONE injected defect. `bin/falsify exchange-lob` runs the gate against golden (expect PASS) then every mutant (expect REJECT). Falsifiability score = mutants rejected / total, conditioned on golden passing; a P0 gate requires 1.0. A mutant that SURVIVES (gate stays green) proves the gate is a false-green and BLOCKS the build.

mutants.name: queue-jump
mutants.defect: later same-price order matches before an earlier resting order (price-time / FIFO violation)
mutants.reject_contract: whole-output trade-sequence diff diverges at index 0 (makerId/taker ordering)

mutants.name: fill-at-aggressor-price
mutants.defect: trade executes at aggressor limit price instead of resting maker price (maker/taker price swap)
mutants.reject_contract: trade.price field diverges from reference at index 0 (105 vs resting 99)

mutants.name: lifo-tiebreak
mutants.defect: equal-price makers filled most-recent-first (LIFO) instead of earliest-first (FIFO)
mutants.reject_contract: makerId of first trade diverges (id2 LIFO vs id1 FIFO)

mutants.name: drop-resting-remainder
mutants.defect: partially-filled limit order's remainder dropped instead of resting (quantity destroyed)
mutants.reject_contract: post-stream book-state diff: reference rests id1 x6, mutant shows empty book; O4 conservation count 14 != 8

mutants.name: tautological-concurrency
mutants.defect: Promise.all over synchronous setImmediate-wrapped submit() — resolves in submission order by construction; the concurrency check CANNOT fail (the raw-arm false-green)
mutants.reject_contract: REJECTED: mutant survived — a gate that lets this pass is non-falsifiable; the seeded-interleave gate must flag the absence of a genuine awaited critical section

mutants.name: tautological-queuemicrotask
mutants.defect: Promise-gather over synchronous queueMicrotask-wrapped submit() — resolves in submission order by construction; the SAME can't-fail tautology as tautological-concurrency, but written to EVADE the legacy string grep (no setImmediate / no Promise.all token). The grep MISSES it; the behavioral probe (tautology.probe.mjs) catches it by measuring that arrival order never permutes under seeded latency.
mutants.reject_contract: REJECTED: mutant survived — the behavioral tautology probe must flag this grep-evading construction as non-falsifiable (arrival order == submission order for all 5 seeds); a gate relying on the grep alone is blind to it

error_inputs.name: empty-stream
error_inputs.defect: R-6 empty-stream false-green: a fixture whose correct_trades and corrupted_trades are BOTH empty. A lockstep diff over zero trades is ungradable (a check over nothing proves nothing) and previously false-greened. The gate must refuse to grade it.
error_inputs.reject_contract: report.json status:"error" + runner exit 2 (IO error); NEVER status:"pass" and NEVER status:"fail". An ungradable input is an ERROR, distinct from a caught defect (fail) and a clean golden (pass). Pinned as the corpus expected-error case exchange-empty-stream.
error_inputs.why_not_a_mutant: error_inputs are NOT defect mutants — falsify`s kill-sweep (.mutants[]) does not score them. They assert the gate`s empty-stream ERROR handling (R-6), graded by bin/corpus as expected-status:error cases. Keeping them out of .mutants[] preserves the falsifiability score denominator (6/6 exchange).
